The Regenerative Farm System

A field guide to regenerative farming and village-scale agriculture

BORNFIDIS ACADEMY

Bornfidis Field Guide No. 01

Contents

1. Introduction ...................... 4

2. Principles of Regenerative Practice ...................... 6

3. Soil and Water ...................... 10

4. Village-Scale Economics ...................... 14

Resources ...................... 18

[Update page numbers after layout.]

Chapter 1

Introduction

This manual is for entrepreneurs, farmers, and community builders who want to align their work with regenerative principles. It outlines the core ideas and practices that support healthy land, strong local economies, and lasting change.

Use it as a field guide: read in order for a full picture, or jump to the sections that match your current priorities.

Who this is for

Whether you run a small farm, a food business, or a village-scale initiative, the framework here is designed to be practical and adaptable. The goal is clarity, not complexity.

How to use this manual

  • Work through one chapter at a time, or use the contents to find a specific topic.
  • Apply the exercises and checklists to your own context.
  • Share sections with your team or community where relevant.

Tip

Keep a notebook or digital doc open while you read. Jot down ideas that connect to your current projects and decisions.

[Continue writing your manual below. Add new chapters by copying the chapter opener (chapter number + H1) and body structure. Use H2 for major sections and H3 for subsections. Use the callout style sparingly for tips, examples, or notes.]
